title: 1981-04-16_Pat_158.txt
key_claims: Dick outlines an evolutionary axis of human emancipation: from the deterministic Babylonian-Greek cosmos, to the rebellious Gnostic worldview, leading finally to the radical autonomy he achieved in 1974.

In this ultimate, unfallen state, the roles of God, Man, and World revert to their proper order: God provides informational advice, Man acts as the empowered creator/regent, and World is the construct shaped by Man.
The Fall occurred when World slipped between Man and God, turning Man into the created object subjected to the physical world, rather than acting as creator of it.
By extracting the divine "Set" out of the Mundane "Ground" via the meta-abstraction, Dick successfully bypassed the world and re-established direct contact with God.
recurring_concepts: **The True Gnosis**: The realization that humanity was originally meant to act as the Logos—the creative intermediary between God and the material world.
reading_excerpt: Dick outlines an evolutionary axis of human emancipation: from the deterministic Babylonian-Greek cosmos, to the rebellious Gnostic worldview, leading finally to the radical autonomy he achieved in 1974.
